title: COSMIC v3.4 SBS signatures rank by mean thermodynamic perturbation; clinical signatures (SBS1 aging, SBS2/13 APOBEC, SBS4 smoking, SBS7 UV) span the loudness spectrum

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'COSMIC v3.4 SBS signatures rank by mean thermodynamic perturbation; clinical sig'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: COSMIC v3.4 SBS signature matrix: 96 trinucleotide channels x 78 signatures.
Per-trinucleotide-channel |delta dG37| (kcal/mol) — magnitude of thermodynamic perturbation for each of the 96 substitution contexts.

operations.note: Per-signature loudness = sum over 96 channels of (signature_weight x |delta dG37|). Pre-normalized signatures so weights sum to 1.

statistics.name: top_10_signatures_by_mean_thermodynamic_perturbation
statistics.note: Top 10 loudest = mostly MMR-deficiency and POLE-driven signatures plus aristolochic acid (SBS22).

statistics.name: clinical_signature_loudness_with_rank
statistics.note: Clinical signatures span the loudness spectrum. SBS22 (aristolochic acid) ranks in the top 10; SBS1 (aging) is in the quiet half.

statistics.name: ratio_max_to_min_signature_loudness
statistics.note: Loudest signature is ~7.5x noisier than the quietest, demonstrating substantial spread across the signature panel.

inference.justification: Single conjunct: the loudest-to-quietest ratio across the 78-signature panel must exceed 3x. This licenses the descriptive 'signatures span the spectrum' framing — without sufficient spread, the per-etiology loudness ranking is not interpretable. The vector-valued statistics (top 10 + clinical subset) are reported but not used in the inference; they describe the ranking that IS the deliverable but aren't testable individually.
inference.failure_mode: If s_loudness_range fell below 3x, the signatures would be tightly clustered in loudness space and per-etiology comparisons would be noisier than meaningful.

conclusion.assertion: Across the 78 COSMIC v3.4 SBS signatures, mean per-signature thermodynamic perturbation (signature-weighted sum of |delta dG37| over the 96 trinucleotide channels) ranges over a 7.5-fold span. Mismatch-repair-deficiency and POLE signatures dominate the top 10 (loudest); SBS22 (aristolochic acid) is the only chemical-exposure signature in the top 10. SBS1 (aging) is in the quiet half. Per-etiology loudness ranking is reported as a per-signature LabeledValue vector.

scope.scale_note: Per-signature aggregate over the standard 96-channel decomposition. SBS39 / SBS40 / etc. are included but omitted from the top-10 list above.

confidence.note: Descriptive ranking; confidence reflects COSMIC v3.4 signature-matrix completeness.

external_assumptions.statement: COSMIC v3.4 signature decomposition is a defensible canonical mutational-process catalog; per-signature weights are normalized within the v3.4 release.
external_assumptions.citation: Alexandrov et al. Nature 2020; COSMIC v3.4 release

external_assumptions.statement: Per-trinucleotide |delta dG37| computed by the PolymerGenomicsAPI biophysics engine is a defensible per-channel loudness metric; alternative thermodynamic measures (Tm, persistence-length perturbation) would shift the ranking but not the spread or the etiology pattern.
